response: Tiv glances up, fingers still sorting through a bin of powder. "Customer or browser? Browsing's fine but don't touch the jars. The sealed ones especially." They tap a jar that appears to contain nothing. "That one bites."

"Shimmerite dust, coppervine wire, sealed clay, void glass shards. The basics." Tiv rattles them off without pausing. "Good quality, all of it. I check every batch myself." They hold up a translucent hand. "Advantage of the biology."

"Everything comes from somewhere else. Different dimensions, different physics. The trick is knowing which ones play nice together and which ones don't." Tiv lines up four containers on the counter. "These four are your starting set. Everything interesting comes from combining them."

"Condensed light. Iridescent powder, bonds permanently with absorbent materials. Clay, fiber, resin — it sinks in and stays." Tiv tips the pouch slightly, and the powder inside catches the light in three colors at once. "Don't put it near void glass. They disagree."

"Wire from a living plant. Still alive, technically — dormant. Conducts vibration, remembers shapes, wraps tight around anything that resonates." Tiv flicks the coil and it shivers. "Void glass resonates at exactly the right frequency. They're natural partners."

"High-pressure dimension. Pre-compressed, stays solid. Mix it with powder and it softens, absorbs, binds permanently." Tiv holds up the waxed-paper package. "Don't get it wet. Water breaks the seal and it foams out. Useless after that. I've seen people ruin a perfectly good lump by sneezing on it."

"From a dimension with almost no light. Hungry for photons — absorbs at the edges, darkens things nearby. Look through it and distances go strange." Tiv holds a shard up, squinting through it with fingers you can see the bones of. "Vibrates near unusual materials. Good detector element. Hates shimmerite though — they push each other away."

narration: You look at the sealed jars on the shelf.
response: "Observer-dependent materials. From a dimension where physics cares whether you're watching." Tiv taps a jar and the contents freeze mid-shift. "Stare at it and it holds still. Look away and it changes. Useful in some applications. Expensive in all of them."

"The smith? Good hands. Terrible organizational system — everything piled on every surface. But the forge is solid work. If Grist says a material will take heat, it'll take heat." Tiv pauses. "They sent you, didn't they? Always sending me customers. I don't mind."

narration: You push off from the counter.
response: "Come back when you need stock." Tiv's already sorting the next bin before you've turned around.
